channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Are European automakers driving into the defense market? Analysts say to tap the brakes. | Breaking Defense
item.description: One expert predicts car companies will be open to defense production “at least as long as substantial funding remains available — especially from the EU, but also potentially from private actors.”

item.title: 🏴 Cheap Missiles, Not Drones, Will Win the Next Air War | War on the Rocks
item.description: After four years of watching the war in Ukraine, NATO defense decision-makers are finally beginning to pour money into drones. The Iran conflict has drawn further attention to these investments. The assumption is that unmanned aerial systems will ensure a long-term advantage in…

item.title: 📍 The Illusion of Sovereignty: How International Law and Big Tech are Eroding the State | War on the Rocks
item.description: Caught between two hammers — international law and technological dependence on the private sector — modern state sovereignty is in crisis. When a state attempts to act decisively against an adversary operating below the threshold of armed attack, it risks not only diplomatic…

item.title: 🌏 Why Kim Jong Un Won’t Pick Up the Phone and What to Do About It | War on the Rocks
item.description: Every American administration pledges to learn from the past when devising a strategy for contending with nuclear North Korea. And yet, few portfolios are so paralyzed by path dependency and resistance to learning. But now, confronted with the inescapable prospect that the…

item.title: 🏴 Dutch startup Intelic sets up drone marketplace for European militaries | Defense News
item.description: BASE will allow defense ministries to explore systems that are ready to be used in a coalition framework, with interoperability guaranteed, says the firm.

item.title: 🕵️ The Indo-Pacific could shape control of the growing spyware market | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The market for commercial cyber intrusion capabilities (CCICs) is moving faster than the frameworks designed to govern it. What began as a niche ecosystem of surveillance vendors has evolved into a sprawling, fragmented industry. While ...

item.title: 📍 Where Are The Carriers As Of May 3, 2026: Ford Finally Heads Home | The War Zone
item.description: Two carrier strike groups remain in the Middle East as President Trump announces ‘Project Freedom’ to get trapped ships out of the Persian Gulf. The post Where Are The Carriers As Of May 3, 2026: Ford Finally Heads Home appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 The End of the Axis of Abraham | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The Arab Gulf and Israel have different visions for a new Middle East.

item.title: 📍 Why the Next India-Pakistan War Will Escalate | Foreign Affairs
item.description: America will struggle to contain it.

item.title: 🌏 Xi’s Forever Purge | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The real goal behind China’s “self-revolution.”

item.title: 📍 Bookshelf: on war and wheat | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Dennis Voznesenski’s War and Wheat examines the complex relationship between grain markets and global conflict. Amid an increasingly precarious global security situation, the book – written by one of Australia’s most respected agricultural economists – ...

item.title: 🌏 Israel To Buy Extra F-15IA And F-35I Squadrons | The War Zone
item.description: After years of high-tempo operations in the Middle East, Israel has locked in plans to further expand its airpower with new F-15IA and F-35I fighters. The post Israel To Buy Extra F-15IA And F-35I Squadrons appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Hedging our bets: a Japanese option for managing risk in the AUKUS Optimal Pathway | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Japan is unlikely to join the submarine component of the AUKUS defence partnership between Australia, Britain and the United States, but it could help mitigate the risks associated with Australia’s acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines. This ...

item.title: 🌍 Israel buying more F-35s, F-15IAs fighter jets, Netanyahu announces | Breaking Defense
item.description: Details are still to come, but Israel plans to buy 25 each of Lockheed Martin’s F-35 and the Israeli version of Boeing’s F-15EX.

item.title: 📍 Could The X-BAT Stealth Fighter Drone Change The Air Combat Game? | The War Zone
item.description: We talk with Armor Harris, the chief designer of X-BAT, about its GE engine, thrust-vectoring controls, Hivemind AI 'pilot' and forthcoming flight-test plans. The post Could The X-BAT Stealth Fighter Drone Change The Air Combat Game? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ 2 US troops reported missing amid African Lion exercise | Defense News
item.description: The Moroccan army said the service members went missing near a cliff. The incident remains under investigation and the search is ongoing.

item.title: 📍 How the Law of War Can Reckon with Longer-Term Harms of Attacks on Health | Just Security
item.description: When war affects complex and interconnected civilian systems, the full measure of civilian harm lies in what comes after the blast. The post How the Law of War Can Reckon with Longer-Term Harms of Attacks on Health appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Digest of Recent Articles on Just Security (Apr. 25-May 1, 2026) | Just Security
item.description: U.S.-Israel-Iran War • U.S.-Cuba • U.S. Boat Strikes • U.S. Military • Transatlantic Relations • Mali • U.S. Political Violence • DOJ Indictment • Anti-Corruption Series • FISA Section 702 • ICE and CBP Operations • Tech Governance • Crimes Against Humanity • Trump Executive…

item.title: 🌏 North Korea Puts Its Medium-Term Plan on Track | The Diplomat
item.description: Unpacking the recent Congress.

item.title: 🏛️ No, The Army Isn’t Fielding New AMPV Armored Vehicles With 30mm Cannons | The War Zone
item.description: BAE has delivered two AMPV 30 variants to the Army to help test them, but the service has no plans to buy this version. The post No, The Army Isn’t Fielding New AMPV Armored Vehicles With 30mm Cannons appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ New VC-25B Air Force One “Bridge” Aircraft Now Fully Modified And Flight Tested | The War Zone
item.description: The ex-Qatari royal flight aircraft is scheduled to be delivered to the Presidential Airlift Group this summer after a warp speed modifications and testing regime. The post New VC-25B Air Force One “Bridge” Aircraft Now Fully Modified And Flight Tested appeared first on The War…

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: I hope you like the color gray! The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 US withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, US officials say | Defense News
item.description: The move comes as a rift over the Iran war widens between President Donald Trump and Europe.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth orders 5,000 US troops to withdraw from Germany | Breaking Defense
item.description: “This decision follows a thorough review of the Department’s force posture in Europe and is in recognition of theater requirements and conditions on the ground,” Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell wrote in a brief statement.

item.title: 🌏 Everything, everywhere, all at once: Japan’s geoeconomic reckoning | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Japanese government has some experience with market interventions, but the current cocktail of issues is taking its toll on borrowing conditions. The post Everything, everywhere, all at once: Japan’s geoeconomic reckoning appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 China’s Massive Stealth Flying Wings Spotted Together At Secretive Test Base | The War Zone
item.description: Testing of China's very large flying-wing drones appears to be ramping up, along with one unmanned fighter-like design. The post China’s Massive Stealth Flying Wings Spotted Together At Secretive Test Base appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Jerome Powell’s legacy of achievements, turbulence, and independence under fire | Atlantic Council
item.description: Both Powell’s major achievements and his shortcomings can serve as lessons for future Federal Reserve chairs. The post Jerome Powell’s legacy of achievements, turbulence, and independence under fire appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Trump: 60-Day War Powers Deadline ‘Totally Unconstitutional’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: The White House argues that the U.S.-Iran cease-fire paused the clock.

item.title: 📍 Will the Next Fed Chairman Be More Compliant With Trump? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Kevin Warsh will face intense pressure to cut interest rates.

item.title: 🏛️ Marine Corps fields 3,500 first-person view attack drones | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Marine Corps had zero first-person view drones in service as of October 2025.
